cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out what the answer is. The question is asking what Lao Yang hoped. The options are about his article being surprising, chosen, liked by children, or making everyone know him. First, I need to look at the story. Lao Yang wanted to be a writer when he was young. He used to send stories to editorial departments but never got any response. Now he works in a factory but still reads when he can. His daughter needs to write an article called "My Father," and he helps her by writing it. He's confident that Miss GAO would like it. Then he asks his daughter if the article was chosen to be sent to the editorial department. The daughter says the teacher said the article digressed from the subject. Lao Yang is angry because he thought he was writing about his father, which is the topic. So, when Lao Yang wrote the article, he probably hoped that it would be selected or chosen by the teacher. Because he had a background of wanting to be a writer and submitting articles before, maybe he wanted his daughter's article to be accepted. The key part is when he asks his daughter if the article was chosen to post to the editorial department. The word "chosen" is directly mentioned here, which is option B. The other options
